title: अष्टलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अष्टलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ आदिलक्ष्मी ॥
सुमनसवन्दित सुन्दरि माधवि चन्द्रसहोदरि हेममये।
मुनिगणमण्डित मोक्षप्रदायिनि मञ्जुलभाषिणि वेदनुते॥
पंकजवासिनि देवसुपूजित सद्गुणवर्षिणि शान्तियुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि आदिलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥१॥

॥ धान्यलक्ष्मी ॥
अयिकलि कल्मषनाशिनि कामिनि वैदिकरूपिणि वेदमये।
क्षीरसमुद्भव मंगलरूपिणि मन्त्रनिवासिनि मन्त्रनुते॥
मंगलदायिनि अम्बुजवासिनि देवगणाश्रित पादयुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि धान्यलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥२॥

॥ धैर्यलक्ष्मी ॥
जयवरवर्षिणि वैष्णवि भार्गवि मन्त्रस्वरूपिणि मन्त्रमये।
सुरगणपूजित शीघ्रफलप्रद ज्ञानविकासिनि शास्त्रनुते॥
भवभयहारिणि पापविमोचनि साधुजनाश्रित पादयुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि धैर्यलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥३॥

॥ गजलक्ष्मी ॥
जय जय दुर्गतिनाशिनि कामिनि सर्वफलप्रद शास्त्रमये।
रथगजतुरगपदातिसमावृत परिजनमण्डित लोकनुते॥
हरिहरब्रह्मसुपूजित सेवित तापनिवारिणि पादयुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि गजलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥४॥

॥ सन्तानलक्ष्मी ॥
अयिखगवाहिनि मोहिनि चक्रिणि रागविवर्धिनि ज्ञानमये।
गुणगणवारिनिधि शरणागतदीन परित्राण परायणते॥
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि सन्तानलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥५॥

॥ विजयलक्ष्मी ॥
जय कमलासनि सद्गतिदायिनि ज्ञानविकासिनि गानमये।
अनुदिनमर्चित कुंकुमधूसरभूषित वासित वाद्यनुते॥
कनकधरास्तुति वैभवन्दित शंकरदेशिक मान्ययुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि विजयलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥६॥

॥ विद्यालक्ष्मी ॥
प्रणत सुरेश्वरि भारति भार्गवि शोकविनाशिनि रत्नमये।
मणिमयभूषित कर्णविभूषण शान्तिसमावृत हासमुखे॥
नवनिधिदायिनि कलिमलहारिणि कामित फलप्रद हस्तयुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि विद्यालक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥७॥

॥ धनलक्ष्मी ॥
धिमिधिमि धिन्धिमि धिन्धिमि दिन्धिमि दुन्दुभिनाद सुपूर्णमये।
घुमघुम घुंघुम घुंघुम घुंघुम शंखनिनाद सुवाद्यनुते॥
वेदपुराणेतिहास सुपूजित वैदिकमार्ग प्रदर्शयुते।
जय जय हे मधुसूदनकामिनि धनलक्ष्मि सदा पालय माम्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री अष्टलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ashtalakshmi Stotram ||

|| Adi Lakshmi ||
Sumanasa-vandita Sundari Madhavi Chandra-sahodari Hemamaye.
Munigana-mandita Moksha-pradayini Manjula-bhashini Vedanute.
Pankaja-vasini Deva-supujita Sadguna-varshini Shantiyute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Adilakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||1||

|| Dhanya Lakshmi ||
Ayi Kali Kalmasha-nashini Kamini Vaidika-rupini Vedamaye.
Kshira-samudbhava Mangala-rupini Mantra-nivasini Mantranute.
Mangala-dayini Ambuja-vasini Deva-ganashrita Padayute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Dhanyalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||2||

|| Dhairya Lakshmi ||
Jaya-vara-varshini Vaishnavi Bhargavi Mantra-swarupini Mantramaye.
Suragana-pujita Shighra-phalaprada Jnana-vikasini Shastranute.
Bhava-bhaya-harini Papa-vimochani Sadhujana-shrita Padayute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Dhairyalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||3||

|| Gaja Lakshmi ||
Jaya Jaya Durgati-nashini Kamini Sarva-phalaprada Shastramaye.
Ratha-gaja-turaga-padati-samavrita Parijana-mandita Lokanute.
Hari-Hara-Brahma-supujita Sevita Tapa-nivarini Padayute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Gajalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||4||

|| Santana Lakshmi ||
Ayi Khaga-vahini Mohini Chakrini Raga-vivardhini Jnanamaye.
Guna-gana-varinidhi Sharanagata-dina Paritrana Parayanate.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Santanalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||5||

|| Vijaya Lakshmi ||
Jaya Kamalasani Sadgati-dayini Jnana-vikasini Ganamaye.
Anudinam-archita Kunkuma-dhusara-bhushita Vasita Vadyanute.
Kanakadhara-stuti Vaibhava-vandita Shankara-deshika Manyayute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Vijayalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||6||

|| Vidya Lakshmi ||
Pranata Sureshwari Bharati Bhargavi Shoka-vinashini Ratnamaye.
Manimaya-bhushita Karna-vibhushana Shanti-samavrita Hasamukhe.
Navanidhi-dayini Kalimala-harini Kamita-phalaprada Hastayute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Vidyalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||7||

|| Dhana Lakshmi ||
Dhimidhimi Dhindhimi Dhindhimi Dindhimi Dundubhi-nada Supurnamaye.
Ghumaghuma Ghunghuma Ghunghuma Ghunghuma Shankha-ninada Suvadyanute.
Veda-Purana-itihasa Supujita Vaidika-marga Pradarshayute.
Jaya Jaya He Madhusudana-kamini Dhanalakshmi Sada Palaya Mam.||8||

|| Iti Shri Ashtalakshmi Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
यह स्तोत्र लक्ष्मी माता के आठ रूपों की स्तुति करता है:

आदि लक्ष्मी - देवताओं और मुनियों द्वारा पूजित, मोक्ष देने वाली, वेदों में वंदित, सद्गुण वर्षा करने वाली शांतिमयी माता।

धान्य लक्ष्मी - कलियुग के कल्मष नाश करने वाली, वैदिक स्वरूपिणी, क्षीरसागर से उत्पन्न, मंगलदायिनी माता।

धैर्य लक्ष्मी - जय और वर वर्षा करने वाली, भय हरने वाली, पाप मोचन करने वाली, साधुजनों को आश्रय देने वाली माता।

गज लक्ष्मी - दुर्गति नाश करने वाली, हरि-हर-ब्रह्मा द्वारा पूजित, ताप निवारिणी माता।

संतान लक्ष्मी - गरुड़ वाहिनी, मोहिनी, चक्रधारिणी, शरणागतों की रक्षा करने वाली माता।

विजय लक्ष्मी - सद्गति दायिनी, ज्ञान विकासिनी, शंकराचार्य द्वारा मान्य माता।

विद्या लक्ष्मी - शोक विनाशिनी, नवनिधि दायिनी, कलियुग के मल हरने वाली माता।

धन लक्ष्मी - दुंदुभि और शंख के नाद से पूर्ण, वेद-पुराण में पूजित, वैदिक मार्ग दर्शाने वाली माता।
benefits: Invokes all eight forms of Lakshmi for complete prosperity
Invokes adi lakshmi (primordial wealth) and dhanya lakshmi (food grains)
Invokes the blessing of dhairya lakshmi (courage) and gaja lakshmi (royal power)
Invokes santana lakshmi (progeny) and vijaya lakshmi (victory)
Invokes the blessing of vidya lakshmi (knowledge) and dhana lakshmi (monetary wealth)
Comprehensive stotra for all-round prosperity and spiritual growth
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Lakshmi, Ashtalakshmi Stotram (अष्टलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Rooted in Traditional, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to comprehensive stotra for all-round prosperity and spiritual growth and bestow Dhairya Lakshmi (courage) and Gaja Lakshmi (royal power). It is also said to bestow Vidya Lakshmi (knowledge) and Dhana Lakshmi (monetary wealth). For best results, devotees chant this on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali and Navratri. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ashtalakshmi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ashtalakshmi Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Hymn to eight forms of Lakshmi for wealth, knowledge, courage, and progeny.
relatedSearches: Ashtalakshmi stotram lyrics Sanskrit eight verses
Adi Dhana Dhanya Gaja Lakshmi forms
Ashta Lakshmi eight forms wealth meaning

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Ashtalakshmi Stotram and when?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashtalakshmi Stotram, as the Wikipedia article on Ashtalakshmi Stotra and Grokipedia's entry both record, was composed in the early 1970s in Chennai by U.Ve. Sri Mukkur Srinivasa Varadachariyar (also referenced in some sources as U.V. Srinivasa Varadachariyar), a theologian of the Sri Vaishnava tradition. The composition is therefore relatively modern in scriptural terms, though it draws on older Pancharatra and Lakshmi Tantra material that names the eight forms of the goddess. The hymn was published by the Sri Ahobila Math and quickly became popular through audio recordings, particularly the M.S. Subbulakshmi rendition. Its rapid acceptance is what drove the contemporary widespread worship of Ashta Lakshmi as a unified set of eight forms, including the construction of the Ashtalakshmi Kovil at Besant Nagar in Chennai dedicated to this specific eight-fold worship.

seoFaqs.question: Who are the eight forms of Lakshmi addressed in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The eight verses of the stotram address, in fixed sequence, Adi Lakshmi (the primordial form), Dhanya Lakshmi (giver of grain and food), Dhairya Lakshmi (giver of courage), Gaja Lakshmi (the elephant-flanked goddess of royal power), Santana Lakshmi (giver of progeny), Vijaya Lakshmi (giver of victory), Vidya Lakshmi (giver of knowledge) and Dhana Lakshmi (giver of wealth). As Drik Panchang's Ashta Lakshmi page, Stotramulu and Hindu Mirror all confirm, this is the canonical order in the Mukkur composition, though some local traditions vary the sequence. The stotram makes the theological point that material wealth (Dhana) is just one of the eight types of abundance Lakshmi grants, with Vidya, Dhairya and Santana being equally important dimensions of true prosperity.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meter and structure of the Ashtalakshmi Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is composed in eight stanzas, one for each form, each set in a flowing meter that allows the verses to be sung melodiously. As Devshoppe's Sanskrit text and Mahakatha's mantra page record, every verse follows a parallel structure: an opening invocation by the form's name, a middle section enumerating her attributes and the boons she grants, and a closing refrain naming her as 'Sri Ashtalakshmi' along with the petition 'palayamam' (protect me). This parallel structure is what gives the stotram its musical accessibility and is why it is commonly chanted by households without Sanskrit training. The total length is short enough (roughly ten minutes when sung at the standard tempo) to fit into a daily devotional routine.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Ashtalakshmi Stotram traditionally chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Stotramulu, Mahakatha and Drik Panchang note that the stotram is most commonly chanted on Fridays (Lakshmi's day), during the Varalakshmi Vrata in the lunar month of Shravana (July-August), throughout Diwali festivities especially on Lakshmi Puja night, on every Purnima, and at the start of any new financial venture or housewarming. Many South Indian households also chant it during the Tamil Pongal harvest festival, since Dhanya Lakshmi (giver of grain) is one of the eight forms invoked. The Friday recitation is often combined with Sri Suktam, Lakshmi Ashtothram and the lighting of a ghee lamp before a Lakshmi image or the Sri Yantra. A common sankalpa is to recite the stotram for eleven Fridays continuously for sustained prosperity.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does each form of Ashta Lakshmi grant according to the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Each verse identifies a specific blessing. As Hindu Mirror and Stotramulu summarise: Adi Lakshmi grants the foundational identity of being graced by the goddess; Dhanya Lakshmi ensures abundance of grain and food security; Dhairya Lakshmi grants courage to face adversity without breaking; Gaja Lakshmi confers royal authority, power and the respect of others; Santana Lakshmi grants progeny and the welfare of children; Vijaya Lakshmi grants victory in undertakings, court cases and competitions; Vidya Lakshmi grants knowledge, wisdom and academic success; and Dhana Lakshmi grants material wealth, gold and steady income. By chanting all eight together, the devotee asks for the complete spectrum of abundance, which is theologically the point of the eight-form structure as opposed to invoking only Dhana Lakshmi.

seoFaqs.question: How did the Ashtalakshmi Stotram make Ashta Lakshmi worship widely popular?
seoFaqs.answer: As both the Wikipedia Ashta Lakshmi article and Grokipedia's entry record, while the eight forms of Lakshmi were known in older Pancharatra and Lakshmi Tantra texts, the unified eight-fold worship of Ashta Lakshmi as a single set was not widespread until the Mukkur composition popularised it in the 1970s. The stotram's accessible meter, the M.S. Subbulakshmi recording, and its adoption by Sri Vaishnava ashramas combined to make Ashta Lakshmi worship a household practice across South India and the diaspora within a single generation. The Ashtalakshmi Kovil at Besant Nagar in Chennai, built in 1976 specifically to enshrine all eight forms, is itself a direct outcome of the stotram's popularity. This makes the hymn one of the few modern compositions to have generated a new pan-regional Devi worship pattern in living memory.
